sentence: The project is believed to have been completed on time
hint: Impersonal passive: subject + is believed/said + to have + pp
explanation: Impersonal passive: 'is believed to have been completed' — reporting structure.

sentence: She had her car repaired at the garage last week
explanation: Causative 'have': subject + had + object + past participle. Someone else did it.

sentence: The suspect is thought to be hiding in the mountains
explanation: Impersonal passive: 'is thought to be hiding' — ongoing reported action.

sentence: He got his phone screen replaced yesterday
hint: Causative with 'get': get + object + past participle
explanation: Causative 'get': more informal than 'have'. 'Got his screen replaced'.

sentence: The new policy is expected to reduce costs significantly
explanation: Impersonal passive: 'is expected to reduce'.

sentence: We are having the house painted this weekend
explanation: Causative present continuous: 'are having the house painted'.

sentence: The company is reported to have lost millions last year
explanation: Impersonal passive: 'is reported to have lost' — past reported event.

sentence: She needs to get her passport renewed before the trip
explanation: Causative with 'get': 'get her passport renewed'.

sentence: The building was being renovated when the earthquake struck
hint: Past continuous passive: was/were + being + past participle
explanation: Past continuous passive: 'was being renovated' — ongoing past action.

sentence: He is said to be one of the best doctors in the country
explanation: Impersonal passive: 'is said to be'.

sentence: They had their luggage checked at the airport security
explanation: Causative 'have': 'had their luggage checked'.

sentence: The results have not been published yet
hint: Present perfect passive: have/has + been + past participle
explanation: Present perfect passive: 'have not been published'. 'Yet' at the end.

sentence: She is getting her hair done for the wedding tomorrow
explanation: Causative 'get' in present continuous: 'is getting her hair done'.

sentence: The ancient city is known to have been destroyed by a volcano
explanation: Impersonal passive + passive infinitive: 'is known to have been destroyed'.

sentence: We should have the contract reviewed by a lawyer
explanation: Causative with modal: 'should have the contract reviewed'.

sentence: The bridge had been closed for repairs since January
hint: Past perfect passive: had + been + past participle
explanation: Past perfect passive: 'had been closed'. 'Since' for point in time.

sentence: He got his suit dry-cleaned before the interview
explanation: Causative 'get': 'got his suit dry-cleaned'.

sentence: The medicine is supposed to be taken twice a day
explanation: 'Is supposed to be taken' — passive obligation.

sentence: They are having a new security system installed next week
explanation: Causative present continuous: 'are having...installed'.

sentence: The painting is believed to have been stolen during the war
explanation: Impersonal passive + passive perfect infinitive: 'is believed to have been stolen'.
